title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
statement_text: For every graph $H$, there exists $\tau>0$ such that every $n$-vertex $H$-free graph has a clique or stable set of size at least $n^{\tau}$.
context_text: A celebrated conjecture of Erdős and Hajnal from 1977, this had been verified for all graphs with at most four vertices by Erdős and Hajnal themselves, and for the bull and $C_5$ among five-vertex graphs, leaving $P_5$ as the last open case. The paper closes this gap by proving the conjecture for $P_5$, completing verification for all five-vertex graphs.
paper_contribution: Proves that $P_5$ satisfies the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture (Theorem 1.2), thereby completing the verification of the conjecture for all five-vertex graphs.
paper_title: Induced subgraph density. VII. The five-vertex path

title: Polynomial Rödl Conjecture (Conjecture 1.4)
statement_text: Every graph $H$ has the polynomial Rödl property.
context_text: Fox and Sudakov strengthened Rödl's theorem by conjecturing that in every $H$-free graph $G$ one can find an $\varepsilon$-restricted induced subgraph on at least $\delta|G|$ vertices where $\delta$ can be taken to be a polynomial power of $\varepsilon$, rather than the previously known quasi-polynomial bounds. This conjecture implies the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture.
paper_contribution: Proves that Conjecture 1.4 holds for $H = P_5$ (Theorem 1.5), which in particular implies the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture for $P_5$.
paper_title: Induced subgraph density. VII. The five-vertex path
